artifacts.reason: The 7201-byte TP8 receipt remains in the private campaign ledger; it records successful graph control, B/C/B-prime, and pristine T followed by a fail-closed raw-speed projection error and zero GPU residue.
A 98-pass focused RTX qualification and protocol matrix was reported during the corrective hardware cycle without retaining the raw log in the repository.
The bounded implementation, adversarial, and confirmation reviews were reported without retaining their raw bytes.

claims.statement: The implementation accepts tied retained top probabilities without inventing a token-order tie break and permits only the validated control prebuild inventory in pristine-reference sessions.
The retained GitHub receipt records successful evidence and CodeQL checks for the implementation head.
A digest-retained TP8 attempt crossed the tied-row and pristine-publication boundaries owned by this change before a distinct post-T speed-evidence inconsistency failed closed.
The retained hardware attempt produced no aggregate PASS, crown, settlement, or weight authority.

exit_criteria.statement: Retained probability rows allow exact ties while continuing to reject nonfinite, duplicate-token, or increasing-logprob rows.
Pristine reference accepts only the exact validator-owned prebuild control inventory and continues to reject candidate native artifacts.
The joined TP8 attempt is not a qualification PASS because raw speed evidence disagreed with device receipt counters after pristine T.
The repository-retained GitHub receipt binds successful evidence checks to the implementation head.

reviews.summary: Implementation review was reported during development; no report bytes were retained.
Adversarial review was reported during development; no report bytes were retained.
Confirmation review was reported during development; no report bytes were retained.
